---
title: Independent-Samples *t*-tests
subtitle: Sample Problems
# author: Guenther, Benjamin A.
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: docs
format: 
  html:
    html-math-method: mathjax
    self-contained-math: false
    embed-resources: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import importlib
import stats_project 
import random

# This is to clear cache and reload after edits to the .py file
importlib.reload(stats_project)
from stats_project import RandomData 
```

# About
the independent-samples *t*-Test problems use the `RamdomData` class which requires:

* the groups variable set to 2: `groups = 2`
* the sample size per group - all groups will have the same sample size
* a call to the `independent_samples_t_test()` method

In these sample problems, the per-group sample size is randomly set between 5 and 15. An example funciton call is included below

```{python}
#| eval: false
sample_size = random.randint(5,15)
RandomData(groups = 2, n = sample_size).independent_samples_t_test()
```

# Problem 1
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
sample_size = random.randint(5,15)
RandomData(groups = 2, n = sample_size).independent_samples_t_test()
```

# Problem 2
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
sample_size = random.randint(5,15)
RandomData(groups = 2, n = sample_size).independent_samples_t_test()
```

# Problem 3
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
sample_size = random.randint(5,15)
RandomData(groups = 2, n = sample_size).independent_samples_t_test()
```

# Problem 4
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
sample_size = random.randint(5,15)
RandomData(groups = 2, n = sample_size).independent_samples_t_test()
```

# Problem 5
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
sample_size = random.randint(5,15)
RandomData(groups = 2, n = sample_size).independent_samples_t_test()
```
